title: rubus hirtus subsp. pectinatus
intro: Rubus hirtus subsp. pectinatus, also known as the thorny raspberry or bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus hirtus plant, a type of wild raspberry native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus subsp. pectinatus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared in various ways, including tea infusions, tinctures, and infusions of the leaves and roots, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
